quickFacts: The entire old town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Einstein developed his theory of relativity while living here in 1905
The Aare river loop makes the old town a natural peninsula

places.name: Zytglogge (Clock Tower)
places.caption: Bern's iconic medieval clock tower
places.description: This 13th-century astronomical clock tower puts on an animated show every hour as jesters, bears, and roosters spring to life. The tower's east face features a stunning astronomical dial. Take a guided tour inside to see the medieval clockwork mechanism up close.

places.name: Aare River Swimming
places.caption: Float through the capital city
places.description: Bern's turquoise-green Aare river is clean enough for swimming, and locals jump in all summer long. The classic route: walk upstream along the Dalmaziquai, jump in, and let the current carry you back downstream. The Marzili pool area is the popular entry point.

places.name: Bern Minster (Münster)
places.caption: Switzerland's tallest cathedral
places.description: Climb the 344 steps of Switzerland's tallest church tower for breathtaking views of the Bernese Alps, the Aare loop, and the red-roofed old town. The elaborate Last Judgment sculpture above the main portal has 234 individually carved figures.

places.name: Einstein House
places.caption: Where relativity was born
places.description: The modest apartment at Kramgasse 49 where Albert Einstein lived from 1903-1905 and developed his theory of special relativity. The preserved rooms and small exhibition give an intimate look at the genius's daily life in Bern.

places.name: Rosengarten (Rose Garden)
places.caption: The best panoramic view in Bern
places.description: This hillside park above the Bear Park offers the postcard-perfect view of Bern's old town — the Aare loop, the cathedral spire, and the Alps behind. With 200+ rose varieties blooming in summer and a restaurant with a terrace view, it's magical at sunset.

places.name: BärenPark (Bear Park)
places.caption: Meet Bern's mascot bears
places.description: Bern is literally named after bears (Bären), and the city has kept bears since 1513. The modern BärenPark along the Aare gives the bears a spacious, naturalistic hillside habitat. Watch them fish, swim, and explore — it's free and open all day.

places.name: Bundeshaus (Federal Palace)
places.caption: Switzerland's seat of government
places.description: The domed Federal Palace houses Switzerland's parliament and offers free guided tours (book ahead). The Bundesplatz in front transforms into a fountain playground in summer (26 jets representing the cantons) and an ice rink in winter.

places.name: Kramgasse
places.caption: Bern's grand medieval main street
places.description: The main artery of the old town, lined with 6 km of arcaded walkways (Lauben) that form one of Europe's longest covered shopping promenades. Ornate fountains from the 16th century dot the street — each tells a different story. The cellars below house bars and boutiques.

places.name: Gurten
places.caption: Bern's local mountain playground
places.description: Take the funicular up Bern's 858m Hausberg for sweeping alpine panoramas, hiking and biking trails, playgrounds, and a revolving restaurant. In July, the Gurten Festival brings big-name music acts to the summit. It's where locals go to escape the city.

places.name: Matte Quarter
places.caption: A hidden riverside neighborhood
places.description: Tucked at the bottom of a cliff below the old town, the Matte quarter sits right on the Aare and feels like a separate village. Once home to artisans and tanners, it now has a bohemian vibe with small cafés, a community garden, and direct river access.

places.name: Zytglogge Clock Tower
places.caption: Medieval astronomical clock
places.description: Bern's iconic 13th-century clock tower with animated figures performing 4 minutes before every hour. A UNESCO landmark.

places.name: Bern Old Town (Altstadt)
places.caption: UNESCO World Heritage
places.description: 6 km of covered arcades (Lauben) with boutiques, cafés and fountains. Medieval cobblestones, sandstone buildings and cellars.

places.name: Rosengarten
places.caption: Rose garden with views
places.description: 220 rose varieties and stunning views over the Aare loop and old town rooftops. Free entry. Lovely restaurant terrace.

places.name: Bundeshaus (Parliament)
places.caption: Swiss Federal Parliament
places.description: Free guided tours of the impressive domed parliament building. The Bundesplatz fountain in front has 26 jets for each canton.

places.name: Bear Park (Bärengraben)
places.caption: Bern's namesake animals
places.description: Open riverside enclosure hosting Bern's famous bears. The bear is the city's heraldic symbol since the 12th century.

places.name: Zentrum Paul Klee
places.caption: Art in a wave
places.description: Renzo Piano's undulating museum holding 4,000 works by Paul Klee. Interactive children's museum too. On the city outskirts.

places.name: Münster Cathedral
places.caption: Switzerland's tallest church
places.description: Gothic cathedral with a 100m tower (344 steps to the top). Incredible panoramic views of the Alps on clear days.

places.name: Einstein Museum
places.caption: Where relativity was born
places.description: Einstein lived in Bern 1903-1905 and developed the theory of relativity here. His apartment at Kramgasse 49 is now a museum.

foodScene: Bern's food scene is cozy, Alpine-influenced, and full of surprises. The signature dish is Berner Platte — a massive platter of various meats (smoked pork, tongue, beef, sausages) served on sauerkraut and beans. Try it at Della Casa, a wood-paneled institution near the Zytglogge. For fondue and raclette, Kornhauskeller serves them in a jaw-dropping Baroque former granary with soaring painted ceilings. The arcaded streets of the old town hide excellent cellar restaurants — Klötzlikeller, dating to 1635, is one of the oldest wine cellars in the country. For modern dining, Restaurant Luce does creative Mediterranean-Swiss fusion. Grab Bernese Mandelbärli (almond bear cookies) from Glatz confectionery. The rooftop bar at Hotel Schweizerhof is the spot for sunset drinks with Alpine views.
